title: Rain Alert for AP, Telangana issued besides heatwaves - The Hans India
full_text:
Rain Alert for AP, Telangana issued besides heatwaves
The Meteorological Department has issued a rain alert for both Telugu-speaking states, warning of impending showers on Monday. A surface trough extending from South Chhattisgarh across Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu has led to the forecast of rainfall. The department has specifically cautioned that thunderstorms with lightning are likely in several districts of Andhra Pradesh, including Anakapalli, Ambedkar Konaseema, Kakinada, Vizianagaram, Srikakulam, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitarama Raju, Polavaram, Chittoor, Tirupati, and Visakhapatnam. The department advised residents to remain vigilant, as rainfall is expected to range from light to moderate.
*Rainfall in Andhra Pradesh*
On Sunday, several areas in Andhra Pradesh experienced rainfall. In Konaseema, Mummidivaram recorded 72.7 millimetres, Peddapuram 50.2 mm, Pithapuram 45.5 mm, and Katrenikona 57.7 mm. Despite the rain in some districts, many parts of the state continued to endure intense heat, with Gudur in Nellore recording a maximum temperature of 46.2°C. Temperatures above 41°C were reported in 286 mandals across the state.
*Weather in Telangana*
Similarly, Telangana saw rainfall in various districts on Sunday, with gusty winds reported in Suryapet, Husnabad, Mancherial, parts of Warangal, Miryalaguda, Neruducherla, and Tripuram. The Meteorological Department expects this weather pattern to persist for the next two days, with several districts likely to receive rainfall. Meanwhile, extreme heat continues to affect the state, with temperatures exceeding 40°C in six districts. Balkonda in Nizamabad recorded the highest temperature at 45.8°C. Tragically, heatstroke deaths are increasing; in Adilabad, Panchayat worker Manik Rao died from heatstroke. The Meteorological Department has issued a warning, urging residents to take precautions, stay hydrated, and avoid outdoor activity during peak heat hours. The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre predicts that the hot conditions will continue for several more days, causing severe distress across North Telangana.
